revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and Structure === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber Structure === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} == External Links == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and Structure === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber Structure === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and Structure === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber Structure === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |築造年代= Late 6th century |埋葬施設= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |被葬者= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]
