No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:シシヨツカ古墳 up to rev 99356060
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= シシヨツカ古墳 |画像=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
所在地地付近 |所在地= 大阪府南河内郡河南町加納地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|scale:20000}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=東西34.5m　南北25.5m |築造年代= 6世紀末前後 |埋葬施設=切石積横穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=象嵌刀装具　小札　鞍金具 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''シシヨツカ古墳'''（ししよつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]加納地内に所在する古墳時代終末期に属する[[方墳]]である。農地整備に伴う事前調査により、存在が確認され、発掘調査された。周辺にある[[ツカマリ古墳]]、アカハゲ古墳などとともに終末期の古墳群（平石古墳群）を構成する。 == 調査の概要== 南河内こごせ地区の農空間整備事業に伴い、当地区周辺には終末期古墳などが点在するため、2001年（平成11年）より大阪府教育委員会によって各所に試掘坑（1メートル×2メートルを111箇所）が設けられ試掘調査が行なわれた。斜面の[[棚田]]に設けられたNo.62試掘坑において、現地表下1.25メートルで天井石2石と版築状盛土層が確認され、古墳の存在することが明らかとなった。地籍に残る字名よりシシヨツカ古墳と命名され、翌2002年（平成12年）より本格調査が行なわれ、墳丘や石室、周濠など、周囲に広がる棚田の一部となって埋没していた古墳のほぼ全貌が明らかにされた。 ===墳丘規模と構造=== 墳丘第2段、第3段に版築状盛土を施す3段築成の横長の方墳である。高さは墳丘裾平坦面から残存墳頂までの比高差は4.7メートルを測る。規模は第1段部分が南北25.5メートル、東西34メートルである。貼石は攪乱された部分を除いて各段の斜面に認められた。また、この貼石は直接斜面に貼りつけるのではなく粘質土を埋込めのように塗りつけてから貼られている。墳丘の周りは周濠で囲んでおり、周濠の幅は北側で8.0メートル、南側で5.5メートル、西側で8.5メートル、東側は出水で損なわれているが9.5メートル程度と見られる。 ===石室構造=== 切石積[[横穴式石室]]で、横口式石槨を奥室とする形状である。奥室、前室、羨道からなり、全長は12メートルである。奥室は右側壁で長さ2.47メートル、左側壁で2.6メートル、幅は1.09メートル～1.12メートル、前室の長さは、右側壁で2.47メートル、左側壁で2.46メートル、幅は1.36メートルある。奥室、前室はすべて花崗岩切石を使用している。奥石は1石よりなり高さ1.32メートル～1.40メートル幅1.08メートル～1.14メートルである。天井石は奥室で1枚（長さ2.47メートル以上）、前室で1枚残存しているが本来は奥室1枚、前室では2枚の構造である。天井石の内法面はきわめて平滑に仕上られている。羨道の長さは約6メートル前後、幅1.5メートル～1.6メートルで、切石を使わず自然石を6～7段に積上げる構造であった。 ===出土遺物=== 石室内の遺物は徹底的に攪乱され、ほとんどは破片となって出土した #[[装身具]]は銀製空玉　瓔珞鎖、金銅製小環、銀製環、銀製帯飾、金銅製指輪、金糸、ガラス玉（総計1067点）があった。 #武器類は鉄刀と刀子の破片と鉄鏃破片多数以外に刀装具として亀甲鳳凰文象嵌円頭大刀柄頭、同鞘尻金具、雲隆文象嵌鞘金具、勾玉巾頸があり、また亀甲繋鳳凰文銀象嵌鞘尻金具の一部と思われる破片もある。 #[[武具]]類は小札甲（[[挂甲]]）に使用されたと思われる大小の小札が出土しており、小札甲4領分に復元できる。 #[[馬具]]類は鞍金具、杏葉、雲珠、辻金具、鏡板がこれもほとんど破片となって出土している。 #棺材は漆塗籠棺破片が出土している。 #土器類は羨道部より[[須恵器]]甕、須恵器高坏が出土しており、年代的には6世紀後半から6世紀末と考えられ、石室、石槨などに切石を使用した[[終末期古墳]]の中では、この古墳を最古の部類にする可能性がある枡本・森川「古墳群」(2009)p.82‐170。 ==造営氏族の推定 == シシヨツカ古墳が造営された平石谷には、この古墳以外にも終末期古墳として[[ツカマリ古墳]]、アカハゲ古墳が存在しそれらも、最近の調査によっていずれも墳丘規模が数十メートルあるのが明らかにされているが、これらの古墳群の被葬者または造営した氏族に関する記録や伝承などはないようである。終末期の古墳としては大規模なものに属するこれらの古墳を３代にわたり造営した主体としては歴史に名の残る人物、氏族であった可能性が高い。その候補に[[蘇我氏]]一族も考えられるが、蘇我氏の河内における墳墓は3kmほど北にある[[磯長谷古墳群]]の中に[[用明天皇]]陵古墳、[[推古天皇]]陵古墳、[[聖徳太子]]墓などともに営まれたようであり、それよりも古墳時代から飛鳥時代にかけて軍事、外交で活躍し、当古墳のある南河内地方にも勢力範囲を持っていたと伝えられる[[大伴氏]]一族を想定する説もある上林「古墳群」(2009)p.398‐。 ==参考文献 == *枡本哲・森川祐輔「シシヨツカ古墳」『加納古墳群・平石古墳群』　大阪府教育委員会　2009年　本文編82‐170頁 *上林史郎「平石古墳群の被葬者像」『加納古墳群・平石古墳群』　大阪府教育委員会　2009年　本文編398‐421頁 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目 == * [[ツカマリ古墳]] * [[金山古墳]] * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)]] ==外部リンク == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm シシヨツカ古墳] （現説公開サイト） * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html 大阪府河南町・シシヨツカ古墳] （大和國古墳取調室） {{デフォルトソート:ししよつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:河南町の歴史]] [[category:方墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[シシヨツカ古墳]] to [[Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= シシヨツカ古墳 |画像=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
所在地地付近 |所在地= 大阪府南河内郡河南町加納地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|scale:20000}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=東西34.5m　南北25.5m |築造年代= 6世紀末前後 |埋葬施設=切石積横穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=象嵌刀装具　小札　鞍金具 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''シシヨツカ古墳'''（ししよつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]加納地内に所在する古墳時代終末期に属する[[方墳]]である。農地整備に伴う事前調査により、存在が確認され、発掘調査された。周辺にある[[ツカマリ古墳]]、アカハゲ古墳などとともに終末期の古墳群（平石古墳群）を構成する。 == 調査の概要== 南河内こごせ地区の農空間整備事業に伴い、当地区周辺には終末期古墳などが点在するため、2001年（平成11年）より大阪府教育委員会によって各所に試掘坑（1メートル×2メートルを111箇所）が設けられ試掘調査が行なわれた。斜面の[[棚田]]に設けられたNo.62試掘坑において、現地表下1.25メートルで天井石2石と版築状盛土層が確認され、古墳の存在することが明らかとなった。地籍に残る字名よりシシヨツカ古墳と命名され、翌2002年（平成12年）より本格調査が行なわれ、墳丘や石室、周濠など、周囲に広がる棚田の一部となって埋没していた古墳のほぼ全貌が明らかにされた。 ===墳丘規模と構造=== 墳丘第2段、第3段に版築状盛土を施す3段築成の横長の方墳である。高さは墳丘裾平坦面から残存墳頂までの比高差は4.7メートルを測る。規模は第1段部分が南北25.5メートル、東西34メートルである。貼石は攪乱された部分を除いて各段の斜面に認められた。また、この貼石は直接斜面に貼りつけるのではなく粘質土を埋込めのように塗りつけてから貼られている。墳丘の周りは周濠で囲んでおり、周濠の幅は北側で8.0メートル、南側で5.5メートル、西側で8.5メートル、東側は出水で損なわれているが9.5メートル程度と見られる。 ===石室構造=== 切石積[[横穴式石室]]で、横口式石槨を奥室とする形状である。奥室、前室、羨道からなり、全長は12メートルである。奥室は右側壁で長さ2.47メートル、左側壁で2.6メートル、幅は1.09メートル～1.12メートル、前室の長さは、右側壁で2.47メートル、左側壁で2.46メートル、幅は1.36メートルある。奥室、前室はすべて花崗岩切石を使用している。奥石は1石よりなり高さ1.32メートル～1.40メートル幅1.08メートル～1.14メートルである。天井石は奥室で1枚（長さ2.47メートル以上）、前室で1枚残存しているが本来は奥室1枚、前室では2枚の構造である。天井石の内法面はきわめて平滑に仕上られている。羨道の長さは約6メートル前後、幅1.5メートル～1.6メートルで、切石を使わず自然石を6～7段に積上げる構造であった。 ===出土遺物=== 石室内の遺物は徹底的に攪乱され、ほとんどは破片となって出土した #[[装身具]]は銀製空玉　瓔珞鎖、金銅製小環、銀製環、銀製帯飾、金銅製指輪、金糸、ガラス玉（総計1067点）があった。 #武器類は鉄刀と刀子の破片と鉄鏃破片多数以外に刀装具として亀甲鳳凰文象嵌円頭大刀柄頭、同鞘尻金具、雲隆文象嵌鞘金具、勾玉巾頸があり、また亀甲繋鳳凰文銀象嵌鞘尻金具の一部と思われる破片もある。 #[[武具]]類は小札甲（[[挂甲]]）に使用されたと思われる大小の小札が出土しており、小札甲4領分に復元できる。 #[[馬具]]類は鞍金具、杏葉、雲珠、辻金具、鏡板がこれもほとんど破片となって出土している。 #棺材は漆塗籠棺破片が出土している。 #土器類は羨道部より[[須恵器]]甕、須恵器高坏が出土しており、年代的には6世紀後半から6世紀末と考えられ、石室、石槨などに切石を使用した[[終末期古墳]]の中では、この古墳を最古の部類にする可能性がある枡本・森川「古墳群」(2009)p.82‐170。 ==造営氏族の推定 == シシヨツカ古墳が造営された平石谷には、この古墳以外にも終末期古墳として[[ツカマリ古墳]]、アカハゲ古墳が存在しそれらも、最近の調査によっていずれも墳丘規模が数十メートルあるのが明らかにされているが、これらの古墳群の被葬者または造営した氏族に関する記録や伝承などはないようである。終末期の古墳としては大規模なものに属するこれらの古墳を３代にわたり造営した主体としては歴史に名の残る人物、氏族であった可能性が高い。その候補に[[蘇我氏]]一族も考えられるが、蘇我氏の河内における墳墓は3kmほど北にある[[磯長谷古墳群]]の中に[[用明天皇]]陵古墳、[[推古天皇]]陵古墳、[[聖徳太子]]墓などともに営まれたようであり、それよりも古墳時代から飛鳥時代にかけて軍事、外交で活躍し、当古墳のある南河内地方にも勢力範囲を持っていたと伝えられる[[大伴氏]]一族を想定する説もある上林「古墳群」(2009)p.398‐。 ==参考文献 == *枡本哲・森川祐輔「シシヨツカ古墳」『加納古墳群・平石古墳群』　大阪府教育委員会　2009年　本文編82‐170頁 *上林史郎「平石古墳群の被葬者像」『加納古墳群・平石古墳群』　大阪府教育委員会　2009年　本文編398‐421頁 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目 == * [[ツカマリ古墳]] * [[金山古墳]] * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)]] ==外部リンク == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm シシヨツカ古墳] （現説公開サイト） * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html 大阪府河南町・シシヨツカ古墳] （大和國古墳取調室） {{デフォルトソート:ししよつかこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:河南町の歴史]] [[category:方墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanan, Osaka|qq=}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga clan|qq=}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|ja|金山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{coordinates|34|29|29|N|135|38|53|E|map_size:20000}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style stone coffin typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

Another theory considers the [[:en:Soga clan|Soga clan]], though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanayama Kofun|Kanayama Kofun]] * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]] == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]][[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shishiyotsuka Kofun]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]
