revision.text:
[[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11307137]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= {{ill|Kanan|WD=Q2305508}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.491389|N|135.648056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style Sarcophagus typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|en|Soga clan|WD=Q909854|lt=Soga clan}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|en|Kanayama Kofun|WD=Q11646971|lt=Kanayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11307137}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|en|Soga clan|WD=Q909854|lt=Soga clan}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|en|Kanayama Kofun|WD=Q11646971|lt=Kanayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11307137}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Shishiyotsuka Kofun |image=[[File:Shishiyotsuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
location vicinity |location= {{ill|Kanan|WD=Q2305508}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.491389|N|135.648056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style Sarcophagus typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|en|Soga clan|WD=Q909854|lt=Soga clan}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|en|Kanayama Kofun|WD=Q11646971|lt=Kanayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11307137}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

location vicinity |location= {{ill|Kanan|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.491389|N|135.648056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type= Square Kofun |area=34.5m east-west, 25.5m north-south |built= Late 6th century |Burial Facility= Cut-stone horizontal stone chamber |Interred Persons= |burial goods= Inlaid sword fittings, lamellar armor, saddle fittings |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} }} '''Shishiyotsuka Kofun''' (シシヨツカ古墳) is a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|WD=Q2305508|lt=Kanan, Osaka|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, Minamikawachi District, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its existence was confirmed through preliminary surveys related to farmland development, followed by excavation. Together with nearby mounds such as {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} and Akahage Kofun, it types part of the Hiraiishi Kofun Group from the terminal Kofun period. == Overview of Excavation == In preparation for agricultural land redevelopment in the Kogose area of Minamikawachi, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education began trial excavations in 2001. A total of 111 test pits (1m × 2m each) were dug across the area. Test pit No. 62, located in terraced rice fields, revealed two ceiling stones and stratified soil layers at 1.25m below the surface, confirming the presence of a Kofun. named Shishiyotsuka Kofun after the local place name, full-map_size excavations began in 2002. These investigations uncovered the mound, stone chamber, and surrounding moat, revealing the structure's near-complete type despite being buried under terraced rice fields. === Mound area and burial === The mound is a three-tiered, elongated square structure with rammed earth layers on the second and third tiers. Its height from the flat base to the remaining mound summit is 4.7 meters. The first tier measures 25.5m north-south and 34m east-west. Stone facing was identified on each tier, though parts were disturbed. The stones were set with clay mortar a11lied to the slopes beforehand. The surrounding moat measured 8.0m wide on the north side, 5.5m on the south, 8.5m on the west, and approximately 9.5m on the east, though flood damage obscured its exact width. === Stone Chamber burial === The burial facility comprises a cut-stone horizontal stone chamber with a horizontal entrance-style Sarcophagus typeing the rear chamber. The chamber system includes a rear chamber, an antechamber, and a passage, with a total length of 12m. The rear chamber walls are constructed of cut granite, measuring 2.47m on the right wall, 2.6m on the left, and 1.09–1.12m in width. The antechamber measures 2.47m on the right and 2.46m on the left, with a width of 1.36m. The rear chamber's ceiling stone is a single piece measuring 2.47m in length, with smooth internal surfaces. The passage, approximately 6m long and 1.5–1.6m wide, is constructed from stacked natural stones without cut-stone finishes. === burial goods === burial goods found in the stone chamber were heavily disturbed and mostly fragmentary: 1. '''Ornaments''': Silver beads, necklaces, gilt-bronze rings, gold threads, and glass beads (1,067 pieces in total). 2. '''Weapons''': Fragments of iron swords, daggers, and arrowheads, including inlaid sword fittings with phoenix motifs, scabbard ornaments, and other fragments suggesting sophisticated craftsmanship. 3. '''Armor''': Lamellar armor fragments sufficient to reconstruct four suits of armor. 4. '''Horse Gear''': Saddle fittings, decorative plates, and other components, primarily in fragments. 5. '''Coffin Materials''': Lacquered wood coffin fragments. 6. '''Pottery''': {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars and pedestal bowls found in the passage, dating to the late 6th century. These suggest that the Shishiyotsuka Kofun may be one of the oldest terminal-period kofun with cut-stone chambers.Masumoto & Morikawa, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 82-170 == Likely Builders == Shishiyotsuka Kofun, along with Tsukamari and Akahage Kofun in Hiraiishi Valley, are large-map_size kofun from the terminal period. No records or oral traditions identify the builders or interred individuals. However, the map_size and construction suggest prominent historical figures or clans. One hypothesis links the tombs to the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, who were influential in military and diplomatic affairs in this region. Another theory considers the {{ill|Soga clan|en|Soga clan|WD=Q909854|lt=Soga clan|1=Soga clan}}, though their Kofuns are concentrated in the {{ill|Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanokawachi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, located 3km north.Kanbayashi, "Kofungun" (2009), p. 398 == References == * Masumoto, Tetsu & Morikawa, Yusuke, "Shishiyotsuka Kofun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 82-170. * Kanbayashi, Shirō, "The Buried Persons of the Hiraiishi Kofungun," in *Kanō Kofungun and Hiraiishi Kofungun*, Osaka Prefecture Board of Education, 2009, 11. 398-421. == notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukamari Kofun|ja|ツカマリ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamari Kofun|1=Tsukamari Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123120421}} * {{ill|Kanayama Kofun|en|Kanayama Kofun|WD=Q11646971|lt=Kanayama Kofun|1=Kanayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|1=Kannonzuka Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.gensetsu.com/020309sisiyotuka/doc1.htm Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Site Report)] * [http://obito1.web.fc2.com/sisio.html Osaka Prefecture Kanan Town - Shishiyotsuka Kofun (Historical Research)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|version=99356060|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:シシヨツカ古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11307137}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
